evals.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS with a 6-person sales team. Our MQL-to-SQL rate is 22% and SQL-to-close is 18%. Marketing sends 200 MQLs/month. The sales team complains MQL quality is low. How do we fix this?
evals.expected_output: RevOps analysis of the MQL-to-SQL gap with recommendations for: MQL definition refinement, lead scoring model improvement, SLA between marketing and sales, and feedback loop design.
assertions: Questions the current MQL definition and scoring model
Recommends a sales-marketing SLA (response time, feedback requirements)
Addresses feedback loop (how sales tells marketing which MQLs are good/bad)
Analyzes the 22% MQL-SQL rate in context (low-moderate, room to improve)
Provides specific lead scoring criteria recommendations

evals.prompt: What metrics should we track in our RevOps dashboard?
evals.expected_output: RevOps metric framework covering the full funnel from MQL through revenue, with recommended targets/benchmarks for each metric for a B2B SaaS.
assertions: Covers the full funnel: MQL, SQL, opportunity, closed-won, revenue
Includes velocity metrics (time in each stage)
Addresses revenue metrics (NRR, ARR growth, churn)
Provides B2B SaaS benchmarks for key metrics
Notes which metrics to look at weekly vs. monthly

evals.prompt: Set up our CRM in HubSpot
evals.expected_output: Offers RevOps strategy input on CRM setup (pipeline stages, property design, automation rules), notes that technical HubSpot configuration is implementation work.
assertions: Provides RevOps strategy for CRM setup (stage design, properties needed)
Notes that technical HubSpot configuration is implementation work
Recommends CRM design principles before touching the tool
